text: This began as a relatively straightforward procedure, a uniportal right lower lobectomy for a peripheral tumor.
At the very beginning of the operation, we started by dividing the pulmonary ligament.
As we progressed, the inferior pulmonary vein became visible.
Dissection continued around the vein, carefully keeping the active blade away to avoid injury.
However, at this point the vein wall was inadvertently grasped, mistaken for connective tissue, the ultrasonic device was activated, resulting in bleeding.
Immediate compression was applied using suction, which was then replaced by a sponge stick for sustained control.
With the bleeding stabilized, we had time to assess the situation.
The video resumes as we attempt to dissect the cranial edge of the vein.
However, we soon realized there was an anatomical variation.
The video is paused again to explain.
The video resumes.
We repositioned the clamp as proximally as possible and attempted to suture the bleeding site.
If successful, this would allow us to preserve the middle lobe vein by separately dissecting the lower lobe vein.
This was technically challenging as the injury was located directly beneath the clamp.
Several sutures were placed but initial attempts did not fully stop the bleeding.
We added additional stitches in a more dorsal and proximal position.
After releasing the clamp again and tightening the sutures, bleeding was successfully controlled.
The knots were secured
and the clots were removed.
With the situation under control, we continued with the lobectomy.
All remaining structures were addressed before returning to the vein.
The lower-low branches of the pulmonary artery were divided from within the fissure.
With each step the anatomy of the middle lobe vein became even more evident.
A tunnel was made to divide the interlobar fissure.
The lower lobe bronchus was then dissected and divided.
Finally, the lower lobe vein was dissected and divided
Please note how the middle lobe vein courses along the ventral surface of the inferior pulmonary vein.
